Marine Biology major is the study of marine organisms and their environment. Students in the Marine Biology major are required to participate in the Marine Biology Quarter (MBQ). The MBQ provides the opportunity for undergraduates to live the life of a marine biologist. Under the guidance of department faculty and graduate assistants, the students design their own research projects, reside and collect data at a field station, conduct experiments to test their hypotheses, and present their work at the end of the quarter.</p><p><br></p><p>Listed below are the lower division major preparation courses for the major as well as important details to review. THIS MAJOR IS HIGHLY SELECTIVE.</p><p><br></p><p>To be considered for this major, you must complete the following courses by the end of spring before transfer:</p><ul><li>One year of Biology with Laboratory,</li><li>One year of General Chemistry with laboratory,</li><li>One year of Calculus, and</li><li>One semester of Organic Chemistry with Laboratory.</li></ul><p>Completion of a second semester of Organic Chemistry or one year of Calculus Based Physics is <strong>STRONGLY RECOMMENDED</strong>. If your college does not offer a two semester or three quarter sequence in Calculus Based Physics, it is recommended that you complete Physics 5ABC at UCLA after you transfer.</p><p>All courses must be taken for a letter grade.</p><p><br></p><p>For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit <u style=\"color: rgb(41, 98, 255);\">https://</u><a href=\"http://www.eeb.ucla.edu/undergraduate.php\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">eeb.ucla.edu/undergraduate.php</a> and <u style=\"color: rgb(41, 98, 255);\">https://</u><a href=\"http://www.admission.ucla.edu\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">admission.ucla.edu</a>.</p><p><br></p><p>PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have been approved to satisfy the preparation requirements for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses listed.</p>","groupId":"28b66f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"<p>UCLA's life science majors require the completion of extensive preparatory coursework prior to transfer, therefore, you must be admitted directly into a life science major; you will not be able to change from a non-life science major into a life science major after admission to UCLA.</p><p><br></p><p>Students who have placed out of UCLA's Math 31A course based on AP/IB credit may be required to complete UCLA's Math 3C, 32A or an equivalent course after transfer.</p><p><br></p><p>The community college statistics course included on this agreement has been approved as a substitute for Statistics 13, however it may not transfer as an exact UCLA equivalent.</p><p><br></p><p>UCLA's Chem 153A is an upper-division course that satisfies a major requirement but is NOT required for admission. After transferring, <strong><u>students must petition</u></strong> for transfer credit and should contact the academic advisor for their major for additional support and details about this process.</p>","groupId":"2bb66f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":3,"area":"General"},{"type":"RequirementTitle","content":"LOWER DIVISION MAJOR REQUIREMENTS","groupId":"482b7745-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"Requirements"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"ADDITIONAL NOTES","groupId":"30b66f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"IMPORTANT MAJOR INFORMATION","groupId":"37b66f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":0,"area":"General"}]
